summary: An Azure Function (Python, Linux Consumption plan) runs on a nightly Timer Trigger to query GPT-4o via TRAPI using Managed Identity (no API keys), then persists the 1985 New York Yankees roster to an Azure Blob Storage container. Infrastructure is provisioned with Bicep. Authentication is fully keyless via Managed Identity and RBAC.
assumptions: TRAPI refers to the Azure OpenAI / AI Foundry token-relay API accessible via Managed Identity Bearer token (no static API keys).
The Azure Function will be implemented in Python 3.11 on a Linux Consumption plan, consistent with existing repository Bicep infra patterns.
The target blob container is 'yankees-roster' in a storage account provisioned via Bicep under /infra.
AzureWebJobsStorage uses identity-based connection (AzureWebJobsStorage__accountName) with no storage account keys, consistent with repo memory.
RBAC roles required: Storage Blob Data Contributor on the yankees-roster container; Cognitive Services OpenAI User (or equivalent) on the Azure OpenAI resource.
CI/CD is delivered via GitHub Actions.
Infrastructure as Code uses Azure Bicep under /infra.
The task plan does not require explicit approval before work begins (no 'auto-approve' flag in spec, so plan approval task is required).
open_questions: What is the exact TRAPI endpoint URL and Azure OpenAI deployment name to use for GPT-4o queries?
Which Azure subscription, resource group, and region should resources be provisioned into?
What is the expected output format of the roster blob (JSON array, CSV, plain text)?
Should prior nightly blobs be overwritten (single file) or appended/versioned with a timestamp?
Are there retry/backoff SLA targets for TRAPI failures or blob write failures?
What alert notification channel should be used (email, Teams, PagerDuty)?
Is there an existing Azure OpenAI resource, or does one need to be provisioned?
What are the performance and uptime SLA targets (spec fields left blank)?

waves.name: Foundation
waves.objective: Establish approvals, core infrastructure, identity/RBAC model, and CI/CD pipeline so all build work can proceed on a solid, secure base.
entry_criteria: spec.md is finalized and committed to main
Azure subscription and resource group target confirmed
TRAPI endpoint and Azure OpenAI deployment name confirmed
exit_criteria: Bicep infrastructure deployed and validated (INF-01 closed)
Managed Identity and RBAC grants verified (SEC-01 closed)
Azure OpenAI / TRAPI access provisioned for Function App identity (INF-02 closed)
GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline deploys successfully to Function App (REL-01 closed)

waves.name: Core Build
waves.objective: Implement the three functional requirements: TRAPI/GPT-4o roster fetch, Blob Storage write, and nightly Timer Trigger orchestration, with structured logging.
entry_criteria: All Foundation wave exit criteria met
Local dev environment configured with Function Core Tools and Python 3.11
TRAPI endpoint URL and Azure OpenAI deployment name available as app settings
exit_criteria: TRAPI client fetches full 1985 Yankees roster and unit tests pass (APP-01 closed)
Blob writer stores roster to 'yankees-roster' container and unit tests pass (APP-02 closed)
Timer Trigger function runs end-to-end locally and in dev environment (APP-03 closed)
Application Insights telemetry and structured logging active (OPS-01 closed)

waves.name: Hardening
waves.objective: Add retry logic, error handling, security validation, and integration/e2e test coverage to ensure production reliability.
entry_criteria: All Core Build wave exit criteria met
Function App deployed to dev/staging environment
exit_criteria: Retry and error-handling logic implemented and tested (APP-04 closed)
Security review confirms no secrets in code or config (SEC-02 closed)
Integration and e2e tests pass in staging environment (QA-01 closed)
Alerting configured for function failures (OPS-02 closed)

waves.name: Release Readiness
waves.objective: Deploy to production, validate the nightly schedule runs successfully, and complete operational documentation.
entry_criteria: All Hardening wave exit criteria met
exit_criteria: Function App deployed to production (REL-02 closed)
Nightly Timer Trigger confirmed firing and roster blob present in production storage (REL-02 closed)

tasks.title: Review and approve implementation task plan
tasks.description: Circulate the generated task plan JSON to the project owner (rciapala) and relevant stakeholders for review and formal sign-off before implementation begins. Confirm open questions (TRAPI endpoint, output format, subscription target) are resolved.
outputs: Resolved open questions documented in spec.md or a decision log
acceptance_criteria: Task plan reviewed by rciapala and at least one engineer
All open questions documented above answered or explicitly deferred with rationale
Plan approval recorded as a comment or issue close on PRJ-01
Any plan amendments committed back to results/task-plan.json
risks: Unresolved TRAPI endpoint details will block INF-02 and APP-01
Missing subscription/resource group confirmation blocks INF-01

tasks.title: Provision core Azure infrastructure via Bicep
tasks.description: Author or update Bicep modules under /infra to provision the Azure Storage Account with 'yankees-roster' blob container, Linux Consumption Function App (Python 3.11), and Application Insights. Use identity-based AzureWebJobsStorage__accountName (no listKeys). Tag all resources with project=1985-NY-Yankees and owner=rciapala. Validate with `az deployment group what-if` before applying.
inputs: Existing /infra Bicep patterns from repo memory
outputs: Deployed Storage Account with yankees-roster container
Deployed Function App with system-assigned Managed Identity enabled
Application Insights instance linked to Function App
acceptance_criteria: Bicep deployment succeeds with zero errors via `az deployment group create`
`az deployment group what-if` shows only expected resource diffs before apply
Storage Account exists with 'yankees-roster' blob container
Function App exists with system-assigned Managed Identity enabled (`az functionapp identity show` returns principalId)
AzureWebJobsStorage__accountName app setting present; no AzureWebJobsStorage connection string with key
Application Insights resource exists and APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING is set on Function App
All resources tagged project=1985-NY-Yankees, owner=rciapala
PR opened targeting main with @copilot assigned for review
risks: Subscription quota limits for Consumption plan in target region
Existing resources may conflict if names are not unique — use deterministic naming

tasks.title: Configure Managed Identity RBAC for keyless Storage and TRAPI access
tasks.description: Extend Bicep RBAC module (/infra/modules/rbac.bicep) to assign Storage Blob Data Contributor on the 'yankees-roster' container scope and Cognitive Services OpenAI User (or equivalent TRAPI role) on the Azure OpenAI resource to the Function App's system-assigned Managed Identity. No API keys, connection strings with secrets, or SAS tokens are permitted.
outputs: RBAC role assignments deployed via Bicep
Verified keyless access from Function App identity
acceptance_criteria: Storage Blob Data Contributor role assigned to Function App identity on yankees-roster container scope (verified via `az role assignment list`)
Cognitive Services OpenAI User (or equivalent) role assigned to Function App identity on Azure OpenAI resource
No storage account keys, SAS tokens, or OpenAI API keys present in any app settings or Key Vault references
`az storage blob list --auth-mode login` succeeds using the Function App identity
PR opened targeting main with @copilot assigned for review
risks: RBAC propagation delay (up to 5 minutes) may cause initial test failures
Cognitive Services OpenAI User role may not exist if using a non-standard TRAPI proxy — confirm correct role name

tasks.title: Provision Azure OpenAI / TRAPI endpoint and app settings
tasks.description: Confirm or provision the Azure OpenAI resource with a GPT-4o deployment accessible via TRAPI. Add TRAPI_ENDPOINT and AZURE_OPENAI_DEPLOYMENT_NAME as Function App application settings (no keys). If TRAPI is an existing shared service, document the endpoint URL and ensure the Function App identity has access. Update Bicep to codify app settings.
outputs: TRAPI_ENDPOINT and AZURE_OPENAI_DEPLOYMENT_NAME app settings on Function App
Azure OpenAI resource (new or existing) with GPT-4o deployment
Bicep updated to manage app settings
acceptance_criteria: TRAPI_ENDPOINT app setting present on Function App (no secret value — endpoint URL only)
AZURE_OPENAI_DEPLOYMENT_NAME app setting present on Function App
Azure OpenAI resource has a GPT-4o model deployment in the target region
No API key app settings present (OPENAI_API_KEY etc. must not exist)
`az cognitiveservices account deployment list` confirms GPT-4o deployment exists
PR opened targeting main with @copilot assigned for review
risks: GPT-4o model availability varies by region — confirm deployment region supports GPT-4o
TRAPI may be a proxy/gateway service with a different auth model than standard Azure OpenAI — needs confirmation

tasks.title: Set up GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline for Function App deployment
tasks.description: Create or update a GitHub Actions workflow (.github/workflows/) to lint, run unit tests, and deploy the Python Azure Function App to Azure on push to main (or on release tag). Use OIDC federated identity or GitHub secret with a scoped Service Principal for deployment — no long-lived credentials. Include a workflow_dispatch trigger for manual deploys.
inputs: INF-01 output (Function App name, resource group)
outputs: GitHub Actions workflow file for CI/CD
acceptance_criteria: Workflow triggers on push to main and on workflow_dispatch
Workflow runs Python unit tests and fails the pipeline if any test fails
Workflow deploys to Azure Function App using OIDC or scoped SP (no long-lived password credentials committed)
First successful end-to-end pipeline run with green status in GitHub Actions
Workflow file linted with actionlint or equivalent
PR opened targeting main with @copilot assigned for review
risks: OIDC federated credential setup requires Azure AD app registration — may need AAD permissions
Zip-deploy size limits on Consumption plan if dependencies are large

tasks.title: Implement TRAPI/GPT-4o client to fetch 1985 NY Yankees roster
tasks.description: Implement a Python module (`trapi_client.py`) that authenticates to Azure OpenAI via Managed Identity (azure-identity DefaultAzureCredential, no API keys), sends a structured prompt to GPT-4o requesting the complete 1985 New York Yankees roster, and returns a parsed list of player names. Include unit tests using pytest with mocked HTTP responses covering success, empty response, and API error cases.
inputs: SEC-01 (Managed Identity RBAC grant on OpenAI resource)
acceptance_criteria: trapi_client.py authenticates using DefaultAzureCredential (no API key usage)
Returns a non-empty list of player dicts/strings representing the 1985 Yankees roster
Unit tests cover: successful roster retrieval, API timeout/error with exception raised, empty/malformed response handling
All unit tests pass: `pytest tests/test_trapi_client.py -v` exits 0 (link test results in PR)
No API keys or secrets present in source code or test fixtures
PR opened targeting main with @copilot assigned for review
risks: GPT-4o output is non-deterministic — prompt engineering needed to ensure consistent, complete roster output
Token quota limits on Azure OpenAI could throttle repeated test calls — use mocks in unit tests

tasks.title: Implement Azure Blob Storage writer for roster data
tasks.description: Implement a Python module (`blob_writer.py`) that authenticates to Azure Blob Storage via DefaultAzureCredential (no connection strings with keys), serializes the roster list to JSON, and uploads it to the 'yankees-roster' container with a configurable blob name (default: 'roster.json' or timestamped variant). Include unit tests using pytest with mocked BlobServiceClient covering success, overwrite, and auth failure cases.
inputs: INF-01 output (Storage Account name, container name)
acceptance_criteria: blob_writer.py uses DefaultAzureCredential; no account key or SAS token in code or config
Roster JSON is uploaded to 'yankees-roster' container as a valid JSON blob
Unit tests cover: successful upload, overwrite of existing blob, missing container error handling
All unit tests pass: `pytest tests/test_blob_writer.py -v` exits 0 (link test results in PR)
PR opened targeting main with @copilot assigned for review
risks: RBAC propagation lag may cause auth failures in early integration testing
Blob overwrite vs. versioning strategy must be decided (open question)

tasks.title: Implement nightly Timer Trigger Azure Function orchestrating roster fetch and storage
tasks.description: Implement the Azure Function entry point (`function_app.py`) with a Timer Trigger using CRON schedule '0 0 0 * * *' (midnight UTC nightly). The function calls `trapi_client.fetch_roster()` then `blob_writer.write_roster()`, logs structured telemetry at each step, and raises exceptions on failure (to enable retry/alert). Unit tests mock both modules and validate orchestration flow, logging calls, and exception propagation.
inputs: Azure Functions Python v2 programming model
acceptance_criteria: Timer Trigger CRON schedule set to '0 0 0 * * *' in function definition
Function calls fetch_roster() and write_roster() in sequence; both calls verified in unit tests
Structured log entries emitted at function start, after fetch, after write, and on error
Unit tests cover: successful end-to-end flow, TRAPI failure propagates as exception, blob write failure propagates as exception
All unit tests pass: `pytest tests/test_function_app.py -v` exits 0 (link test results in PR)
Function runs successfully with `func start` locally using local.settings.json (no secrets committed)
PR opened targeting main with @copilot assigned for review
risks: Timer Trigger does not retry on failure by default — retry must be handled in host.json or app logic
Cold start latency on Consumption plan may cause timer to fire slightly late

tasks.title: Configure Application Insights structured logging and distributed tracing
tasks.description: Ensure the Function App emits structured logs and distributed traces to Application Insights using APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING (set in INF-01). Configure host.json logging level, add correlation IDs to log messages in trapi_client and blob_writer, and validate telemetry appears in Application Insights Live Metrics and Logs.
inputs: INF-01 output (Application Insights connection string app setting)
outputs: Structured logs visible in Application Insights
Trace correlation in Application Insights transaction search
acceptance_criteria: APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING app setting present and non-empty on Function App
Function invocation logs appear in Application Insights under 'traces' within 5 minutes of a test run
Structured log fields (function name, invocation ID, step name) present in log records
No sensitive data (tokens, keys, roster PII if applicable) present in log payloads
Live Metrics stream shows active function invocations during manual test trigger
risks: Connection string vs. instrumentation key: use connection string (instrumentation key deprecated)
Log sampling may drop entries under high volume — verify sampling settings

tasks.title: Add retry logic and error handling to TRAPI client and blob writer
tasks.description: Implement exponential backoff retry (max 3 attempts) for transient TRAPI HTTP errors (429, 503, 504) in trapi_client.py and transient Azure Storage errors in blob_writer.py. Add dead-letter logging (structured error log with full exception context) when retries are exhausted. Update unit tests to cover retry behavior and exhaustion paths.
outputs: Updated trapi_client.py and blob_writer.py with retry logic
Updated unit tests covering retry scenarios
acceptance_criteria: TRAPI client retries up to 3 times with exponential backoff on 429/503/504 responses
Blob writer retries up to 3 times on transient storage errors
Exhausted retry scenarios log a structured error message with exception details
Unit tests for retry: success on 2nd attempt, failure after 3 attempts, non-retryable error not retried
All unit tests pass: `pytest tests/ -v` exits 0 (link test results in PR)
PR opened targeting main with @copilot assigned for review
risks: Over-aggressive retry on TRAPI could exhaust token quota — cap total retry duration
Retry on blob write without idempotency check could cause duplicate partial writes

tasks.title: Security review: validate no secrets in code, config, or history
tasks.description: Run a secrets scanning pass using `git log` inspection, truffleHog or GitHub secret scanning, and manual review to confirm no API keys, connection strings with credentials, SAS tokens, or passwords are present in source code, app settings templates, or git history. Verify all auth uses Managed Identity / DefaultAzureCredential.
inputs: All source files in function app
acceptance_criteria: GitHub secret scanning shows zero open alerts on the repository
truffleHog or equivalent scan returns no high/critical findings
local.settings.json (if committed) contains only placeholder values, not real secrets
.gitignore includes local.settings.json
No OPENAI_API_KEY, storage account key, or SAS token found in any file via `grep -r`
All auth code paths use DefaultAzureCredential confirmed by code review
risks: Secrets accidentally committed in early development — git history rewrite may be needed
local.settings.json inadvertently committed without .gitignore entry

tasks.title: Execute integration and end-to-end tests in staging environment
tasks.description: Deploy the Function App to a staging slot or dev environment and manually trigger the Timer Function via `func azure functionapp logstream` or the Azure Portal 'Run now' button. Verify the roster blob is created in the 'yankees-roster' container with valid JSON content listing all 1985 Yankees players. Automate the blob content assertion in a pytest integration test.
outputs: Roster blob in staging yankees-roster container
acceptance_criteria: Manual trigger of Timer Function completes without error in staging
Blob 'roster.json' (or equivalent) exists in 'yankees-roster' container in staging storage
Blob content is valid JSON and contains at least 20 player entries (1985 Yankees had 25-man roster)
Application Insights shows successful invocation trace with no exceptions
Integration test `pytest tests/integration/ -v` passes and results linked in QA-01 close comment
All prior unit tests still pass after integration (`pytest tests/ -v` exits 0)
risks: GPT-4o response may omit players — prompt tuning may be needed
Staging RBAC may differ from production — validate independently

tasks.title: Configure alerting for nightly function failures
tasks.description: Create Azure Monitor alert rules (via Bicep or Azure Portal) on the Application Insights resource to fire when the nightly function invocation fails (exception count > 0) or when no successful invocation is recorded between 00:00–01:00 UTC. Route alerts to a configured notification channel (email or webhook).
inputs: Notification channel details (email/Teams webhook — open question)
outputs: Azure Monitor alert rule for function failure
Azure Monitor alert rule for missing nightly execution
acceptance_criteria: Alert rule 'FunctionFailure' fires when exceptions > 0 in a 5-minute window during nightly window
Alert rule 'MissingNightlyRun' fires when no successful function invocation in 00:00–01:00 UTC window
Action group routes notifications to configured channel (email confirmed delivered in test)
Test alert fires correctly when manually triggered via 'Test action group' in portal
Alert rules codified in Bicep or ARM template and committed to /infra
risks: Alert notification channel not yet defined (open question) — blocks action group creation
Consumption plan cold start may cause function to run at 00:01–00:02 UTC, adjust alert window accordingly

tasks.title: Deploy to production and validate nightly schedule
tasks.description: Execute production deployment via the GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline (REL-01) targeting the production Function App. Verify the Timer Trigger fires on the first nightly schedule, the roster blob is written to production 'yankees-roster' container, and Application Insights shows a clean invocation trace. Confirm alerts (OPS-02) are active.
acceptance_criteria: GitHub Actions pipeline deploys to production Function App with green status
Timer Trigger fires at or near 00:00 UTC on the first night after deployment
Roster blob exists in production 'yankees-roster' container with valid JSON content
Application Insights shows zero exceptions in the nightly invocation trace
OPS-02 alerts are active and monitoring the production Function App
No API keys or secrets present in production app settings (verified via `az functionapp config appsettings list`)
risks: Consumption plan cold start on first nightly trigger after deployment
Production RBAC propagation delay if resources were just provisioned

tasks.title: Author operational runbook for nightly roster function
tasks.description: Write a runbook (docs/runbook.md) covering: architecture overview, infrastructure components, how to manually trigger the function, how to read Application Insights logs, how to re-run after a failed nightly execution, how to rotate/update TRAPI endpoint configuration, and escalation contacts.
acceptance_criteria: docs/runbook.md committed to main and accessible in the repository
Runbook covers: architecture diagram or description, manual trigger steps, log query for last nightly run, re-run procedure, TRAPI endpoint update procedure, escalation contacts
Runbook reviewed and approved by rciapala
All links in runbook resolve (Azure Portal links use resource name not hardcoded IDs where possible)
risks: Runbook may become stale if infrastructure changes — link to Bicep outputs rather than hardcoding resource names
